title: Module 1
content:
Basic Structure of computers – functional units - basic operational
concepts - bus structures. Memory locations and addresses - memory
operations, Instructions and instruction sequencing,  addressing modes.
Basic processing unit – fundamental concepts – instruction cycle –
execution of a complete instruction - single bus and multiple bus
organization Register transfer logic: inter register transfer – arithmetic,
logic and shift micro operations.

title: Module 2
content:
Processor Logic Design: - processor organization – Arithmetic logic
unit - design of arithmetic circuit - design of logic circuit - Design of
arithmetic logic unit - status register – design of shifter - processor unit
– design of accumulator.
Arithmetic Algorithms: Algorithms for multiplication and division
(restoring method) of binary numbers. Array multiplier, Booth’s
multiplication algorithm.

title: Module 3
content:
Pipelining: Basic principles, classification of pipeline processors,
instruction and arithmetic pipelines (Design examples not required), hazard
detection and resolution I/O Organization: accessing of I/O devices –
interrupts, interrupt hardware -Direct memory access. Control Logic
Design: Control organization – Hardwired control-micro program control –
control of processor unit - Micro program sequencer, micro programmed
CPU organization - horizontal and vertical micro instructions.

title: Module 4
content:
Memory System: basic concepts – semiconductor RAMs. memory
system considerations – ROMs, Content addressable memory, cache
memories - mapping functions. Hardware Software Design and
Embedded Product Development: Hardware software co design,
modeling of systems, embedded product development life cycle
management, life cycle models, Product design, design process , testing.
Suggestions on Project Topics
1. Ripple Carry Adder
2. Carry Look Ahead Adder
3. Wallace Tree Adder
4. Synthesis of Flipflops
5. Register and Counters
6. Combinational Multipliiers
7. Booths Multiplier
8. ALU
9. Memory Design
10. Associative Cache Design
11. Direct Memory MappedDesign
